figures.caption: Table 4. Representation-level improvements induced by SSDM-based augmentation in downstream QML tasks (RQ4). Results are reported for n = 2, 4, 6 qubits.
Figure 2. Training stability across system sizes n ∈ {2, 4, 6} (rows). Columns report training loss, F0, MMD, ∆obs, and entropic Wasserstein distance (Ent. W1).
Table 1. Main results on n = 2, 4, 6 qubits. Higher is better for fidelity F0, while lower is better for MMD, ∆obs, and entanglement Wasserstein distance.

Table 3. Ablations on local-time supervision and score construction for n = 2, 4, 6 qubits (RQ3). Higher is better for fidelity F0, while lower is better for MMD, ∆obs, and entanglement Wasserstein distance.
Figure 1. Illustration of the forward diffusion on the quantum pure-state manifold and its reverse-time generative process driven by a learned Riemannian score.
